file_insights.purpose: Data class representing barcode scan results from MT90 scanner

properties.name: barcodeType
properties.description: The type of barcode scanned (e.g., EAN13, CODE128, QR_CODE)

file_insights.purpose: Updated with Android/Gradle build artifacts and IDE configuration patterns

patterns_discovered.description: Creation of lightweight data class for modeling domain objects with minimal properties
patterns_discovered.context: ScanResult models a single scan operation result with barcode and type information

patterns_discovered.description: Project follows standard Android development structure with Gradle build system
Using String type for barcodeType instead of enum or sealed class
patterns_discovered.implications: Flexible but lacks type safety at compile time

gotchas_discovered.description: barcodeType is defined as String, allowing any value and losing compile-time type checking
gotchas_discovered.context: Examples given (EAN13, CODE128, QR_CODE) suggest bounded set of values

gotchas_discovered.description: Data class accepts any String values without validation of format or content
gotchas_discovered.context: Barcodes typically have format requirements depending on type

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created ScanResult data class with required properties (barcode and barcodeType)

recommendations.recommendation: Consider using an enum or sealed class for barcodeType
recommendations.rationale: Would provide compile-time type safety and prevent invalid barcode types; examples suggest finite set of allowed values
recommendations.example: enum class BarcodeType { EAN13, CODE128, QR_CODE, ... }

recommendations.recommendation: Add input validation for barcode field
recommendations.rationale: Barcode formats are typically constrained by their type; validation could happen in secondary constructor or factory function
Scanner data likely needs to be transmitted over network or persisted; Kotlin Serialization or @Parcelize would be beneficial
recommendations.example: @Serializable data class or @Parcelize for Android Bundle support

recommendations.recommendation: Document expected barcode formats by type
recommendations.rationale: KDoc comments mention examples but don't specify format constraints or validation rules
recommendations.example: Add format specifications to property documentation
